mnemonics: FLP: Forget Losing Patience (The FLP Impossibility result states it's impossible to have a perfect consensus algorithm in an asynchronous system if even one process can fail. Don't lose patience trying to design one!)
keyPoints: Consensus algorithms (like Paxos and Raft) are fundamental for getting multiple, potentially faulty, processes to agree on a single value.
Logical Clocks (Lamport & Vector) provide a way to order events in a distributed system without a global physical clock.
Leader election is a specific type of agreement problem crucial for managing single points of coordination to avoid conflicts.
commonMistakes: Assuming a synchronous network model (reliable and bounded delay) when designing algorithms, which is an unrealistic simplification for real-world systems.
Ignoring the CAP theorem trade-offs; believing a distributed system can simultaneously provide perfect Consistency, Availability, and Partition tolerance.
Confusing the roles in Paxos (Proposer, Acceptor, Learner) and misunderstanding how a majority is required for each phase of the protocol.
